ops.title: tilth rip-and-replace supersedes per-op peel-off verdict
ops.body:
Supersedes "tilth verdict: per-op peel-off, not replacement" (2026-07-13). That note's escalation trigger was grammar drift; the actual failure was tilth's own stale index (read --section labeled a line 11 off while native Read was right; grep agreed — the third staleness band-aid territory after the grep zero-recheck and content anchors).

Decision 2026-07-16: replace ALL tilth ops natively — compose ast-grep + ripgrep + git/jj (no tree-sitter, no go/packages); de-vendor ast-grep to a normal PATH dependency with a 0.44.0 version floor; redesign output shapes (goldens re-cut, house invariants stay); flatten router/Backend to an internal/dispatch table with semble as the only MCP-session engine; delete internal/vendor, internal/grep, internal/grok's fallback dance, and all three tilth regex grammars (render/finalize.go, render/diff.go, cli/history.go). Nothing native caches across calls — staleness-by-construction.

Four staged releases starting v0.22 (v0.21.0 was already taken by edit --match). Plan file: ~/.cc-pool/accounts/acct-02/plans/bash-cc-notes-papercut-streamed-brook.md. Upstream PRs jahala/tilth#184-188 become moot for ccx; open tasks 777edc9 + 50ae868 die as side effects (close when their phases land).
